position_summary: Claude’s v1.1 revision fixed several high-risk issues in substance: the DOI is now repo-consistent, the liveness split is explicit in the outline, the program-hop tautology is surfaced, and role/model/checkpoint independence is scoped correctly. However, direct repo verification shows v1.1 is not yet internally self-consistent: stale headline/demo-scope sentences still overclaim the ledger, retain CTLA4, and blur the disease hop back into the experimental substrate framing.

findings.title: CTLA4 was removed from C17/Table 2 but still remains in the outline’s top-level ledger scope
findings.evidence: Verified in `docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md`: C17 now says CTLA4 REMOVED and Table 2 says CTLA4 dropped (`OUTLINE.md:182`, `OUTLINE.md:230`). But the earlier Demonstration scope still says the ledger includes `SLC1A5+CTLA4 refuted` (`OUTLINE.md:33`).
findings.concern_path: A drafter following the outline’s high-level scope can reintroduce CTLA4 as a headline refuted exemplar despite the corrected C17 source note saying CTLA4 is supported in the perturbseq-qc source.
findings.suggested_action: Remove CTLA4 from the Demonstration scope sentence, or rewrite it as `SLC1A5 refuted; CTLA4-at-Rest only if explicitly scoped to the separate e2e artifact`.

findings.title: The ledger is still said to prove discrimination before being scoped as demonstration evidence
findings.evidence: The headline contribution still says `the verdict ledger proves the referee discriminates` (`docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:30`). Later §4.1 correctly scopes it as `demonstration evidence` and `NOT as a validated precision/recall benchmark` (`OUTLINE.md:119-121`).
findings.concern_path: The outline tells the eventual manuscript to make a strong method-validation claim in the opener, then retracts it in Results. Reviewers will notice the stronger first framing even if the caveat appears later.
findings.suggested_action: Change the headline sentence to `the verdict ledger demonstrates referee discrimination on worked examples`; keep the optional negative-control panel as the route to a stronger sensitivity claim.

findings.title: The primary-artifact rule landed in prose but not in the C1-C10 source rows
findings.evidence: The §7 header says C1-C8 source of truth is `stage1/sweep_Stim8hr.json` and C9-C10 is `stage1/lbd_questions_Stim8hr.json` (`OUTLINE.md:205-210`). But C1-C8 still cite the finding doc / README / `same`, and C9-C10 cite README/finding doc, not the JSON files (`OUTLINE.md:214-223`). The JSON artifacts do contain the values, e.g. `sweep_Stim8hr.json` has `a_genes=3935`, `eligible_pairs=22039`, `ab_gate_value=26`, and class splits in its first 25 lines.
findings.concern_path: The source-of-truth contract is split between header and table. A manuscript build from the table can drift back to prose-summary citations instead of primary receipts.
findings.suggested_action: Update the C1-C10 table rows themselves to cite `stage1/sweep_Stim8hr.json` and `stage1/lbd_questions_Stim8hr.json` directly, with prose docs only as corroboration.

findings.title: A stale headline sentence still implies every hop is adjudicated against the experimental substrate
findings.evidence: §2.2 now correctly says KD/effect/program are backed by the held experimental substrate while the disease hop is an association/enrichment receipt, not experimental proof (`OUTLINE.md:81-85`). But the headline contribution still says deterministic tools adjudicate each hypothesis `against a held experimental substrate with a receipt at every hop` (`OUTLINE.md:27-30`).
findings.concern_path: The later disease-hop calibration is good, but the first-viewport thesis still lets a reviewer read the disease receipt as part of the experimental oracle/substrate rather than GWAS-enrichment association.
findings.suggested_action: Rewrite the headline contribution to name both receipt classes: perturbation-substrate receipts for KD/effect/program and association/enrichment receipts for disease.

findings.title: Primary JSON artifacts still contain uncalibrated `validated` language while the outline plans to cite them as receipts
findings.evidence: `docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage1/lbd_questions_Stim8hr.json` still has `referee_overall: consistent with a validated gene -> program -> disease chain...` for NAB2 and other rows. The outline’s §4.5 says manuscript-facing labels must avoid `validated` and §7 points C9-C10 to this JSON family as primary artifacts.
findings.concern_path: Even if the manuscript text is calibrated, reviewers opening the cited receipt JSON see the exact word the Sonnet critic supposedly forced out, weakening the self-audit story.
findings.suggested_action: Either regenerate/sanitize the receipt JSON wording to `receipt-backed` / `consistent with`, or explicitly mark this artifact as pre-critic/internal and cite a post-critic receipt for manuscript-facing evidence.

escalated_findings.rationale: Partially addressed but not closed. CTLA4 was removed from Table 2 and C17, but a stale high-level Demonstration scope sentence still says `SLC1A5+CTLA4 refuted`.
Partially addressed. §4.1 now scopes the ledger as demonstration evidence, but the opening headline still says the ledger `proves` discrimination.
Partially addressed. The §7 header names primary artifacts, but the actual C1-C10 table rows still cite prose summaries or `same` instead of the JSON receipts.

Partially addressed. §2.2 correctly separates perturbation substrate receipts from disease association receipts, but the headline still says every hop is adjudicated against the held experimental substrate.
Partially addressed. The outline adds a manuscript-facing calibrated-language rule, but primary JSON artifacts that the outline plans to cite still contain `validated` language.

dropped_findings.rationale: Resolved by repo verification. Exact search found no remaining `10.1101/2025.12.23.696273`; `references/README.md`, `README.md`, `MEMORY.md`, `WORK_PROGRESS.md`, and the outline now use `10.64898/2025.12.23.696273`.
Addressed. §3.3 and §4.1 now explicitly state the program-hop tautology caused by `program_significant=True` and avoid presenting the funnel program hop as independent evidence.
Addressed in the outline. §3.4 and §4.5 distinguish full-scale cached-receipt replay from the separate live 12-gene microsweep and external cross-model audit.
Addressed. The outline defines self-audit independence operationally as role/model/checkpoint independence and reserves cross-model independence for Codex/external replication.
Addressed. §5.1 now scopes the R01 answer to follow-up triage/adjudication against a held substrate, not replacement of prospective wet-lab follow-up.

recommended_next_question: Round 3 should verify a small cleanup pass: remove the stale CTLA4 and `proves` wording, rewrite the headline receipt-class sentence so the disease hop is not experimentalized, and decide whether to sanitize or quarantine primary JSON artifacts that still say `validated`.
